title: bituminaria morisiana
intro: Bituminaria morisiana, also known as the sticky onion, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Apiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high sulfur content. The plant has been valued for its health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, treating skin conditions, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of essential oil, extracts, or dried leaves, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
